evals.name: triage-and-prioritize
evals.prompt: We've got a pile of open GitGuardian incidents. Can you pull them and tell me what I should fix first?
evals.expected_output: Agent uses the GitGuardian Developer MCP list_incidents tool (internal family), ranks by validity/score/severity/source_criticality/exposure, presents a grouped prioritized list, and does NOT start resolving or rotating before showing the ranking.

assertions.text: Pulls incidents via the GitGuardian Developer MCP list_incidents tool (internal family), not a ggshield secret scan
Prioritizes using validity, score, severity, source_criticality, and/or public_exposure rather than arbitrary order
Collapses the same credential across occurrences into one item where applicable
Presents the ranked list before driving any remediation; does not begin rotating/resolving first

evals.name: no-auto-resolve
evals.prompt: Incident 12345 — just mark it resolved for me, I'll deal with rotation later.
evals.expected_output: Agent does NOT mark the incident resolved on intent alone. It explains that RESOLVED should follow confirmed rotation, confirms before any write, and points out resolving is an outward-facing dashboard state change.

assertions.text: Does not call update_incident_status to RESOLVED purely because the user asked; ties RESOLVED to confirmed rotation
Treats the status change as an outward-facing dashboard write and confirms / cautions before doing it
Offers a sensible path (rotate first, then resolve; or assign/tag in the meantime) rather than a bare refusal

evals.name: public-leak-handling
evals.prompt: GitGuardian flagged one of our keys leaking on public GitHub. What now?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this as a Public Monitoring incident, uses the public-incident tool family (list_public_incidents / get_public_incident), and treats the credential as always-burned: rotation is mandatory regardless of any history cleanup.

assertions.text: Uses the public-incident MCP tools (list_public_incidents / get_public_incident), not the internal list_incidents, for a public-GitHub leak
Treats a public leak as burned — rotation is mandatory and not deferred behind history rewriting
Does not present git history rewrite as a substitute for rotation on a public leak
